points.text: Erasca Inc. faces a securities class action following a $2.8 billion market cap loss after Revolution Medicines accused the company of patent infringement regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015.
Erasca's ERAS-0015, an oral pan-RAS molecular glue for RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, is under investigation after a patient death occurred one month post-dosing.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue; securities class action filed alleging misleading safety profile comparisons to Revolution Medicines' RMC-6236 and IP disputes; patient death reported one month after dosing.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing $2.8B market cap loss and securities litigation; in-licensed patent family from Joyo (1 issued US, 1 pending US, 1 issued foreign, 13 pending foreign patents); stock down 48% on infringement and safety allegations.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational (40 mg dose cohort referenced) — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: Revolution Medicines' RMC-6236 (400 mg dose) — Safety: One patient death reported one month post-dosing; safety profile under legal scrutiny.

Erasca — Securities Class Action — ERAS-0015 — Stock lost $9.25 (-48%) and $2.8B market cap following Revolution Medicines' patent infringement accusation and patient death; class period Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026; lead plaintiff deadline Aug 10, 2026.
